name: Habitat for Humanity of Lee and Hendry Counties, INC.
description_short: Non-profit housing organization providing affordable homeownership programs, HUD-certified housing counseling, and financial stability support to low-income families in Lee and Hendry Counties since 1982.
description_long:
Habitat for Humanity of Lee and Hendry Counties, Inc. is a non-profit organization that has been serving families in Southwest Florida since 1982. The organization focuses on eliminating barriers to better, healthier, and more financially stable lives through affordable housing solutions and homeownership programs. It operates under the broader Habitat for Humanity mission but serves a specific geographic region: Lee and Hendry Counties.

The organization offers multiple pathways to financial stability and homeownership. Their core homeownership program partners with qualified, low-income families to build or acquire decent, affordable homes. They provide HUD-certified housing counseling through one-on-one sessions for individuals seeking to finance, maintain, or own homes. Additionally, they operate Habitat ReStore, a donation-based retail store where proceeds support home building. The organization also runs Women Build, a program specifically designed to engage women in affordable housing efforts, and offers virtual information sessions about the pathway to homeownership available in both English and Spanish.

What distinguishes Habitat for Humanity of Lee and Hendry Counties is their comprehensive approach combining counseling, hands-on partnership, and community engagement. They achieved a 4-star rating from Charity Navigator, indicating strong organizational effectiveness. In 2024-25, they counseled 731 families on homeownership and facilitated 62 family home purchases. Their HUD-certified housing counselors provide professional guidance, and their volunteer-driven model creates community investment in outcomes. The organization offers free donation pickup for ReStore items and free virtual information sessions, removing financial barriers to access.

This organization is best suited for low-income families genuinely seeking stable, affordable homeownership rather than quick credit fixes or emergency cash. The primary caveat is that their homeownership program requires applicant qualification (based on income and other criteria), involvement of family "sweat equity" in building, and time commitment. They are not a lender themselves but rather a non-profit partner facilitating the path to ownership through counseling and community-supported construction.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselors provide professional, one-on-one homeownership guidance
4-star Charity Navigator rating demonstrates organizational credibility and financial responsibility
Counseled 731 families on homeownership in 2024-25, showing significant community impact
Virtual information sessions available in both English and Spanish for accessibility
Free donation pickup service for ReStore items removes barriers to participation
Comprehensive approach combining counseling, affordable housing, and volunteer community engagement
62 families purchased homes in 2024-25 through their partnership model
cons: Homeownership program requires family qualification based on income and other criteria—not all applicants are approved
Families must invest significant 'sweat equity' (work hours) in building process, which requires time commitment and physical ability
Limited to Lee and Hendry Counties geographic area—not available for families outside this region
Housing counseling is advisory only; organization does not directly provide financing or credit repair services
Website does not clearly specify timeline expectations for moving from application through home purchase
best_for: Low-to-moderate income families seeking stable, long-term affordable homeownership in Lee or Hendry Counties
Individuals needing HUD-certified housing counseling to prepare for homeownership or mortgage qualification
Families willing to participate in community-based building and commit time to the partnership model
Women-led households interested in Women Build programming and peer support during homeownership journey
diagnosis: Best for low-income families in Lee and Hendry Counties seeking genuine long-term affordable homeownership with professional counseling support. Primary caveat: requires family qualification, meaningful time/labor commitment, and realistic timeline expectations—this is not a quick credit fix or emergency loan product, but rather a comprehensive partnership approach to stable housing.
services: HUD-certified one-on-one housing counseling for homeownership preparation
Affordable homeownership programs for qualified low-income families
Virtual information sessions on pathway to homeownership (English and Spanish)
Habitat ReStore donation center and resale retail store
Free donation pickup service for new and gently used items
Women Build program for engaging women in affordable housing efforts
Volunteer opportunities for community members to support home building
Housing maintenance and financial stability counseling
Home dedications and community celebration events
Habitat neighborhood development and strategic community planning
Career opportunities for passionate individuals in housing field
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 12751 New Brittany Blvd Ste 100, Fort Myers, FL 33907
